description: Calculate net commute flows and classify zones as labor importers or exporters in San Francisco using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/commute_flow_analysis.ipynb. Using data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, determine the resident_workers (sum of workers), local_jobs (count), net_commuters (workers - jobs), and worker_job_ratio (workers / jobs) for each zone. Classify zones into 'commute_role' categories based on net_commuters: major_importer (<= -5000), moderate_importer (-5000, -1000], balanced (-1000, 1000), moderate_exporter [1000, 5000), and major_exporter (>= 5000). Export the results to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_commute_flows.csv with columns zone_id, resident_workers, local_jobs, net_commuters, worker_job_ratio, and commute_role. Create a summary JSON at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/commute_summary.json with keys: total_citywide_workers, total_citywide_jobs, num_major_importers, num_major_exporters, max_net_importer_zone, max_net_exporter_zone, and pure_bedroom_communities (zones with >1000 resident_workers and <100 local_jobs). Save a scatter plot of Local Jobs vs Resident Workers to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/commute_scatter.png and ensure all cells are executed.
